title: Present Perfect vs. Simple Past
subtitle: Grammar of the day
body: This is one of the most common mistakes Spanish speakers make. The key is whether the time is specified or still relevant to now.

examples.translation: experiencia de vida — tiempo no importa

examples.word: I saw that movie last Friday.

tip: If you can answer 'when?', use Simple Past. If 'when?' is irrelevant, use Present Perfect.
